provenance.rationale: Round 01 (n=18) produced a single-dimensional picture: param_importances.rag_top_k=1.0, rag_use_mmr=0.0, rag_query_parser=0.0. Top cluster (13 trials at mrr 0.8778) covers rag_top_k in [7,14]; bottom cluster (5 trials at 0.8667) covers rag_top_k in {3,5}. boundary_hits.rag_top_k.high=0 so no reason to expand; .low=2 hits are both in the worse cluster so narrowing is safe. Both categoricals frozen at their WorkerSettings defaults (rag_use_mmr=false, rag_query_parser=off) — the plateau splits 5/5 and 6/4 across their choices so either value is indistinguishable from the other in the metric, and freezing shrinks the confirmation round to 9 effective int cells. n_trials=15 because this is a confirmation sweep of a narrowed space, not fresh exploration. reviewer.kind=human / approved_at=null because freezing 2 of 3 search dims is a large structural change even if the importance math says it is safe.

diff_summary.evidence: clusters[1].trial_numbers span rag_top_k in {3,5} at mrr 0.8667; clusters[0].trial_numbers span rag_top_k in [7,14] at mrr 0.8778; statistics.boundary_hits.rag_top_k={low:2, high:0}; param_importances.rag_top_k=1.0
param_importances.rag_use_mmr=0.0 at n=18 (was 0.032 at n=8, now confirmed noise); clusters[0] splits 5/5 between false and true
param_importances.rag_query_parser=0.0; clusters[0] splits 6/4 between off and regex; regex normalization is a no-op on this fixture's well-formed KR queries

notes: Confirmation round on a narrowed single-axis space. Expected result: best value stays at 0.8778, rag_top_k importance stays at 1.0, stop_conditions.no_improvement_rounds fires (will be 1 after this round). Round_03 plan: promote rag_reranker to categorical ['off', 'cross_encoder'] after BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3 is prewarmed in the HF cache, or swap the fixture to something with more headroom than 0.8778-mrr / 0.9667-hit@5. The two sentinel __FILL_AT_ADAPTER__ hashes in the schema docs are NOT used here because I computed real sha256s off the on-disk bundle and config.yaml directly.
